programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: A new series from Woodgreen Pets Charity and more heart-warming tales of lost dog meets new owner. Can glam Poodle-cross Lola live up to the expectations of five-year-old Violet?

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Sarah Beeny's New Life in the Country
programme.desc: The family enjoy some time away together as they barge down the Kennet and Avon Canal, explore the hills of Avoncliff on bike and swim with an urban mermaid.

programme.title: Narrow Escapes
programme.desc: Josh and Hayley's boat, 'Darth Vader', powers up for its journey from Lincoln to Milton Keynes: may the course be with them! Can Gen stay afloat balancing dreams with deadlines?

programme.title: Sun, Sea and Selling Houses
programme.desc: Following two British families who have set up shop in Spain and are selling property to Brits looking for a bit of fun in the sun.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: John and Karly dream of owning a holiday home on Spain's Costa Almería for all their family to enjoy. Lee Juggurnauth shows them what their £90k can buy.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Barbara and Leigh met in Cyprus years ago, and now they want a holiday home there to suit the whole family. Can Ben show them a property that's an ideal match?

programme.title: Chateau DIY: Australia
programme.desc: Can Deanne and Richard bring a bit of Brigitte Bardot to their Bordeaux château? Near Périgueux, Lia and Richard's kitchen walls are falling down...

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Craig Rowe helps Maria and Paul find a property that feels like a home away from home among Andalucia's countryside and traditional pueblos blancos. For their budget of £270,000 Craig shows them five great properties.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: First to host in York is motivational master and training guru Steve Carlyle, whose `sophisticated' dinner party, complete with butler and posh nosh, does anything but boost his reputation.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's day two in York, and eccentric ghost tour walker Trevor Rooney throws a `Fright Night' themed evening in a haunted bric-a-brac warehouse.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Next to host in York is hippy hostess Annie Albericci who hopes to bring peace, love and harmony to the table with her `festival chic' themed evening, but Steve Carlyle threatens to ruin the mood.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Pint-sized nurse Yvonne McGill hosts a `black and white swinging 60s' dinner party on day four in York but worries that straight-talking Steve will ruin the evening for everyone.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's a night of flirting and friction on the final day of the competition in York, as foodie perfectionist Rebecca Ryan throws a Hollywood themed evening of glitz and glamour, with some help from `Peggy' and `Audrey', her kitchen appliances.

programme.title: Will My Summer Holiday Be Cancelled?
programme.desc: Dispatches asks: what's happening with our summer holidays since the Iran War sent jet fuel prices soaring and caused thousands of flights to be cancelled? Will this mean holidays are out of the question this summer? Kate Quilton investigates.

programme.title: Bake Off: The Professionals
programme.desc: Hosted by Liam Charles and Stacey Solomon, 'Bake Off: The Professionals' welcomes professional pastry chefs, some of whom have experience of working for some of the United Kingdom's top hotels, restaurants and businesses, to compete in a series of baking challenges. From intricately designed miniatures, to show-stopping tarts and cakes, judges Cherish Finden and Benoit Blin make sure the teams use all of their skills to produce a variety of delicious desserts, knowing that failure to do so can result in elimination from the competition.

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Young Henry has been accidentally hit by a forklift truck driven by his dad. Best mates Dean and Nahum are reeling from a car crash. And 94-year-old Pearl has badly cut her arm by trapping it in a lift.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Britain's busiest air ambulance teams return, bringing the hospital to the patient, from Lanchester to Ashdon, and a motorbike accident in the Peaks.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: The celebs hit the sofa for a new series. What do Rylan and his mum, Tom Allen, Mo Gilligan, Vicky Pattison, Denise van Outen, and many others, think of this week's TV?

programme.title: First Dates
programme.desc: Two 19-year-olds take their first steps into the dating world together. A hip-hop artist meets another musician. And can weightlifter Jack give bodybuilder Eloise a lift?

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: When Clive meets Diane at work, the chance of a romance leads him to confess secrets that he's kept quiet for years. But when Clive and Leo discover the truth about Calico Road, they're thrown into open warfare.

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: Leo and Clive stand alone, face-to-face, in the ultimate confrontation on a night of terror. Meanwhile, Zee reaches out to help George, and they embark on a joyous voyage of discovery that will have terrible consequences for everyone.

programme.title: Tip Toe
programme.desc: Clive's house becomes a tinderbox of raw emotion as George's secrets spread across town, and even Saul can't stop the onslaught of anger. Zee desperately tries to help, but Leo does exactly the wrong thing as violence explodes on Calico Road.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britain's sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the week's biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Interceptor Martin clocks a driver smashing the speed limit at more than 100mph. Soon banged to rights in the back of the cop car, the driver gets lippy and his attitude lands him in even more trouble.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: When his girlfriend is taken away, a drunk driver shows his true colours by propositioning Interceptor Sophie in the back of the cop car. An Astra van leads the interceptors on a breathless, night-time pursuit with devastating consequences.

programme.title: Vengeance Valley
programme.desc: After returning from a cattle drive, Colorado ranch foreman Owen Daybright and his foster brother, Lee Strobie, learn that unmarried Lily Fasken is about to give birth. Owen goes to visit her and discovers she hasn't much support, so he gives her some money. Her brother, Dick, suspects that Owen is the father and eventually Owen is driven out of town.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A gang of masked men exit a cloned car being tracked by cops. Intel suggests they're here to rob a cannabis factory and police must approach with extreme caution.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: The agents investigate the mysterious deaths of Chinese immigrants whose bodies were missing various internal organs.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera

programme.desc: An insight into this rarely seen world of high stakes, colourful characters and extreme conditions.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: When a heavy lorry becomes embedded in a ditch after careering off the M1 and plunging down an embankment, Steve must work out how to free the truck whilst preventing it from falling on its side.

programme.title: Trucking Hell

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: interceptor Steve is involved in a dangerous high-speed pursuit of a car stolen in a burglary. The fleeing driver ditches and runs, but will the police chopper be able to keep him in their sights?

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Dogged determination helps Dan to track down a driver who has given him the slip in the side roads of Spalding, and Suzie comes to the rescue of a motorist with a rather large passenger.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The dog unit embark on a desperate manhunt amid fears that a weapon-wielding brawler is roaming the streets of Lincoln. Craggy closes in on a shop thief with a suspicious bag full of surprises.

programme.title: Motorway Cops: Catching Britain's Speeders
programme.desc: Featuring a dangerous high-speed chase through Newcastle city centre, and a serious crash between a car and a cyclist with some uncertainty about who was at fault.

programme.title: Ice Road Truckers
programme.desc: On her first solo trip, Steph Custance braves one of the longest ice crossings on the map; Darrell Ward attempts a triple delivery in a single day; an engine fire stops Lisa Kelly in her tracks; Todd Dewey celebrates his birthday stuck on a road.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Mulder and Scully must sort through differing stories while investigating a couple who claim to have been abducted by aliens.

programme.title: Seconds From Death: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: A snowboarder gets stuck in a very perilous position and a cliff jumper survives against the odds.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: Steve Grahame tasks Slick with a one last desert run just before Christmas. And, Chaz Butt embarks on a mercy mission to farmers in drought-ravaged New South Wales.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In Northumberland, a heavy tipper gets lost in the dark and ends up badly stuck in a boggy field. Meanwhile, recovery op Zibby gets the keys to a brand-new set of wheels in Leicester.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: In the north of England, Kevin is called to a particularly challenging recovery involving a heavy crane that has become stranded in the remote countryside during the middle of the night.

programme.desc: Following work at a specialist clinic where four top dermatologists treat a range of conditions. They squeeze, drain or pop cysts, lipomas and anything else that's getting under their patients' skin.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachel's Inadvertent Kiss
programme.desc: Rachel has an interview for her dream job at Ralph Lauren. Meanwhile, Monica competes to have a better relationship than Phoebe's, and Joey is arrested for stalking a sexy neighbour with whom Ross gets a date.

programme.title: Bargain-Loving Brits in the Sun
programme.desc: Holiday makers and engaged couple Lolly and Merv research their retirement dream in Benidorm. And busy mum Sophie begins training for the London Marathon.

programme.title: Car Pound Cops: Give Me My Car Back!
programme.desc: Following the dramatic work of recovery crews as they trace, track and tow vehicles driven illegally on Britain's roads. With access to two recovery companies, featuring the action from the moment they are called in, to the trucks arriving on scene.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Exploring how criminals use some of the Derbyshire's fastest roads, including the M1. In this episode, it takes six units from two police forces to stop a suspected cloned van.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: A suspicious car linked to multiple thefts crashes into passing traffic, and the cops battle a surge in thefts from lorries - they intercept and box in a couple of known offenders.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Car thieves go out of control and on the run and a young driver has a brush with death.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Kev Salter and Ian Squire hunt for a man roaming the late-night streets with a machete. The driver of a feisty Fiesta makes off from the cops and takes the pursuit off-road.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Interceptor Damien Stevens draws his stun gun on an aggressive driver, the hunt for a suspected armed robber turns up a nasty surprise for dog handler Jimmy Greaves, and Paul Jackson grapples a man with a machete.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Damo Stevens hunts down a heroin dealer, Jacko rushes to a critical car crash, and the Interceptors investigate some potentially hazardous goods. Meanwhile, a young driver makes a mistake that she will come to regret.

programme.title: Call The Bailiffs: Time To Pay Up
programme.desc: Following the work of enforcement agents as they execute High Court writs on behalf of people desperate to recover the money they are owed. When defendants are tracked down, agents face tense confrontations and dilemmas.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: Alf is firm with Leah and Justin - he won't be driving again until the real culprit who clipped Abigail is found.

programme.title: Home and Away
programme.desc: John hands Mali the invite to Archie's birthday, insisting Mali attend, and Mali can let Tane know they've opened the gym against his wishes.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Dr Emily Crosse sees Pratibha, who is concerned about her failure to lose weight despite only eating salads. John has come in after his optician flagged up that skin tags on his eyelids are affecting his vision.

programme.title: Casualty 24/7: Every Second Counts
programme.desc: Patient numbers are very high as the team are facing one of their busiest days. Keeping the team going through this high pressured shift is the self-named Emergency Clown Sister Jane, who takes the younger nurses under her wing and boosts morale.

programme.title: 999: Emergency Call Out
programme.desc: For the first time in Britain, police and paramedics are joining forces to respond to 999 calls for help, using state-of-the-art fast response cars to be the first on scene at the most challenging and complex incidents.

programme.title: 999 Critical Condition
programme.desc: Cases include a builder who has had a 25-foot fall through a roof and sustained spinal fractures, a woman having an acute asthma attack, and a pregnant cancer patient undergoing surgery.

programme.title: Skin A&E
programme.desc: Insight at a specialist clinic where a team of top dermatologists treats a range of conditions.

programme.title: Scam City
programme.desc: Conor Woodman visits Barcelona, one of the most popular tourist destinations in the world and home to a skilled set of conmen and thieves.

programme.title: GPs: Behind Closed Doors
programme.desc: Teenager Elizabeth, who is being treated for an eating disorder, has recently started vomiting involuntarily, so she and her mother speak to Dr Syed in search of an answer. Mohammed thinks the worst about his headaches and chest pains.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A serial offender gives officers the runaround, a high-speed pursuit comes to a painful finish, the occupants of a 4x4 need rescuing after overturning on treacherous ice, and the interceptors are called on a mucky mercy mission after two of their own get caught in a sticky situation.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Angus and Sean have to smash down a door in order to save a man's life, veteran interceptor Gordon picks up a gruesome twosome on the M6, and a fleeing Land Rover rams two cop cars in a bid to get away.

programme.desc: Angus and Sean have to smash down a door in order to save a man's life, veteran interceptor Gordon picks up a gruesome twosome on the M6, and a fleeing Land Rover rams two cop cars in a bid to get away.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel's new boss and colleague both smoke. When Rachel discovers that they make business decisions without her in the smoking room, she pretends to be a serious smoker so she can be part of the discussion. Meanwhile, Ross's son and Joey audition for the same commercial, but only one of them can get the part.

programme.desc: Mealladh Breatannach a chuidich leis an ionnsaigh air Sicily ann an 1943. How a darkly humorous British ruse helped the 1943 landing in Sicily.

programme.title: Brunch le Abigail

programme.title: The Call of the Isles

programme.title: Rurachd (Foraging)
programme.desc: Mary Ann Kennedy and Lucy Cooke hunt the good-for-you plants and the ones that definitely aren't.

programme.title: Dàna: Scotland's Wild Side
programme.desc: Coinneach Rankin and Somhairle Johnston face unexpected challenges in their search for adventure on Spain's Costa Blanca.

programme.title: Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox begins his exploration of the cosmos with a hymn to the great luminous bodies that bring light and warmth to the universe: the stars. It is estimated that there are two hundred trillion stars in the universe, each playing their part in an epic story of creation, a great saga that stretches from the dawn of time, with the arrival of the first star, through diverse generations until the arrival of our own star, the Sun, and a civilisation that has grown up in its light.

programme.title: Laurence Rees Remembers: The Nazis - A Warning from History
programme.desc: Over 40 years on from the end of the Second World War, this landmark series explored the reasons why Germany fell in thrall to the Nazis. Combining astonishing archive, storytelling and interviews with figures at the heart of Hitler's rise to power, it tells us of the Nazi mentality, why they believed what they did.

programme.title: The Nazis: A Warning From History
programme.desc: Unearthing the shocking truth about how a cultured nation at the heart of Europe allowed a monster like Hitler to come to power. This film reveals how the Nazi party was born, and how, by January 1933, Germany voted for a Chancellor who was openly committed to overthrowing democracy.

programme.title: Ken Loach Remembers-The Old Oak
programme.desc: Ken Loach reflects on The Old Oak, his deeply moving portrait of solidarity, displacement and community in a former mining village in County Durham. Set against the arrival of Syrian refugees in a town scarred by austerity and deindustrialisation, the film explores whether compassion and collective action can survive in fractured times.

programme.title: The Old Oak
programme.desc: A pub landlord in a previously thriving mining community struggles to hold onto his pub. Meanwhile, tensions rise in the town when Syrian refugees are placed in the empty houses in the community.

programme.title: Ken Loach Remembers-Up the Junction
programme.desc: Ken Loach discusses Up the Junction, his landmark 1965 contribution to the BBC's The Wednesday Play strand, adapted from Nell Dunn's celebrated stories of working-class life in Battersea. Blending drama with documentary realism, the production shocked audiences with its frank depiction of sex, illegal abortion and everyday poverty, attracting record complaints while redefining television drama in Britain.

programme.title: The Wednesday Play
programme.desc: Classic play by Nell Dunn about three working-class young women who live, work and play in Battersea.

programme.title: This Cultural Life
programme.desc: John Wilson speaks to leading figures from the worlds of art and culture to hear about the formative influences and inspirations that fired their creative imaginations.

Former police inspector Anton Sullivan takes a trip to a garden centre to highlight simple tips that can deter wannabe burglars. And Janet turns Doorbell Detective to foil an elaborate scam when she receives a brand new mobile phone.

programme.sub-title: Well Worth the Wait in Cornwall
programme.desc: An old Sunday School in Cornwall undergoes a heavenly transformation. Two other unique properties in Hampshire and Lancashire are up for auction with interesting plans afoot.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt

programme.desc: Steve Brown is with a buyer who has a £425,000 budget to spend on a new house in her childhood home county of Devon. On his travels, Steve also visits an organic, family-run orchard, which produces award-winning cider.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie revisits a garden in Northampton that was designed to be accessible for a couple's disabled children. We also find out what's become of a theatrical-themed garden in Gosport.

programme.title: Sort Your Life Out
programme.sub-title: Is It a Bedroom, Or Is It a Garage?
programme.desc: Stacey and her team head to East Sussex to help the Jamieson family tackle their overflowing converted bungalow. programme.title: Food Fest Scotland
programme.desc: Edith Bowman introduces Colin Murray to her home town of Anstruther on a food adventure. As well as its famous fish and chips, they discover how artisan bread is being made for the community. Farmer Jim Smith tests his tastebuds with some hot sauces, and discovers the secrets of marmalade. Chef Julie Lin is in Dundee finding out how bakers keep up with demand around the clock.

programme.title: Born to be Wild
programme.desc: Head of birds Lorraine is one of the most experienced and dedicated members of the SSPCA rescue centre staff, every year hand-rearing hundreds of baby nestlings and nursing dozens of injured birds back to health.

programme.title: Icons of Football
programme.desc: Legendary goalkeeper Craig Gordon grants behind-the-scenes access to his life over 12 months as he makes one last attempt to reach a World Cup, at the age of 42.

programme.title: Kevin Bridges: In Search of the Beautiful Game
programme.desc: Kevin Bridges embarks on a journey to see if the beautiful game still exists. He meets Brazil football icon Cafu, Scottish legend John McGinn, São Paulo ultras, US football fans and many others.

programme.title: That Was the Team That Was
programme.desc: Scotland's 1974 World Cup team was arguably the best group of Scottish footballers ever assembled and were considered genuine contenders to win the tournament. This is the inside story of that unforgettable summer of football told by the players who represented Scotland in West Germany.

programme.title: Grand Tours of Scotland's Rivers
programme.desc: Paul explores the upper reaches of the river Spey - Scotland's third longest river - from its source in the wilds of Badenoch to Broomhill, halfway down the river. He hears stories of clan chief Cluny Macpherson who hid with his gold for nine years.

programme.title: Beechgrove Garden
programme.desc: Beechgrove Garden is full of information for gardeners across the country who have to cope with the colder climates of the UK.

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie is on Montrose Beach, measuring the increasing rate of coastal erosion. Meanwhile, as the annual clip gets under way, Cammy gives apprentice Anne a masterclass in the art of shearing.

programme.title: Highland Cops
programme.desc: Police on Skye raid an abandoned house they suspect is being used to grow cannabis and deal with a three-car accident which causes chaos on the island's main road.

programme.title: Scotland Stands Up
programme.desc: Join Farmer Jim Smith for a fantastic night of stand-up comedy, featuring Susie McCabe, Jamie MacDonald, Stuart McPherson and Marjolein Robertson. It's a cracking show with some of the most exciting comedians on the circuit today.

programme.title: Scot Squad
programme.desc: While helping a blind man do some shopping, Volunteer Officer Ken Beattie discovers there's more to him than meets the eye. Something that also applies to the old pensioner that Officers McLaren and Fletcher rush to rescue.

programme.title: Life On the Bay
programme.desc: Manager Steven has to deal with a pungent problem when the Fife caravan park's swimming pool filter needs to be changed. It's hot work for him and maintenance team members Billy and Gibby, who lend a hand.

programme.title: WA Diamond League Athletics
programme.desc: Live coverage as a host of athletics stars, including Mondo Duplantis and Karsten Warholm, compete in Oslo in the sixth leg of the 2025 Diamond League. A sell-out crowd at the famous Bislett Stadium will be hoping Olympic pole vault champion Duplantis can once again set a new world record. Meanwhile, world champion and former Olympic 400m hurdles champion Warholm will have the home support as he takes on Rai Benjamin.

programme.title: The Catch-Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: A daring night mission causes group tension and isolates Pip. Jamie's case stalls until Pip decodes a clue that cracks it wide open. In court, the verdict is given.

programme.title: A Good Girl's Guide to Murder
programme.desc: As they chase the truth, Pip and her friends face new risks and shocking discoveries. Will they find Jamie and make it through unharmed?

programme.title: Escape Room: Tournament of Champions
programme.desc: Six people unwittingly find themselves locked in another series of escape rooms, slowly uncovering what they have in common to survive -- and discovering they've all played the game before.

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam is back at his parents' house in Leeds. Having told Jess he'll spend the day preparing to perform at a local spoken word night, instead he's procrastinating and playing old video games.

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam is doing cocaine at a warehouse party with a stranger on a Tuesday night. He should be preparing for his mental health assessment. Has he always been this reckless?

programme.title: Ladhood
programme.desc: Liam lies to Jess about missing his mental health assessment and tensions in their relationship reach boiling point. Liam is suddenly nostalgic for the simplicity of first love.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: The boys attempt to find a trio of cars which are equally at home on the race track as they are at transporting a family to the shops, all for a budget of £5,000. Plus, James makes a rare visit to the `Top Gear' track to test a pair of Porsches, the 911 Sport Classic and Boxster Spyder, while Alastair Campbell is the star in the all new Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Life is hard, so Callum and Jordan, two emotionally-unassembled young lads from Northern Ireland, start the Funboys' Fun Club: a playgroup for childish young men to engage in wholesome mucking about. But when a juice party goes awry, Callum's ultra-religious fiancée Morgan kicks them out, and the Funboys must search for a new HQ.

programme.title: This Is BBC TWO
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: An air ambulance pilot with a taste for adventure is searching for a peaceful Northern Ireland home where she can truly switch off after high-flying shifts. Originally from New Zealand, Agnieszka has lived an extraordinary life, including time spent in Antarctica, and now has £200,000 to find a place of her own within an hour of Belfast airport. Helping with the search is Briony May Williams, who also squeezes in a spirited Irish dancing lesson on the scenic shores of Lough Neagh.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Chris Hull and Lee Burkhill travel to Coventry to help mother-and-daughter nurses Barbara and Tate transform their completely empty garden into an exotic jungle retreat.

programme.title: Great Japanese Railway Journeys
programme.sub-title: Japanese Alps: Nagano to Narai Juku
programme.desc: Michael ends the first leg of his Japan adventure on a Shinkansen bullet train, heading to Nagano, the host city of the 1998 Winter Olympics and the gateway to the Japanese Alps.

programme.title: Great Korean Railway Journeys
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, Michael travels to South Korea's south-west, where food, protest and landscape reveal the values that have shaped the modern nation.

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: Twenty-four hours a day, the latest national and international stories as they break.

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Engaging conversation about the political issues that matter to you.

programme.title: Live Tennis: ATP & WTA Queen's 2026 LIVE
programme.desc: Live coverage from day 3 of the 2026 Queen's tennis tournament.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Every day this week, celebrity contestants Simon Armitage, Josie Gibson, Ardal O'Hanlon and Adele Roberts take on a series of quiz rounds selected by the host, quiz legend Richard Osman.

programme.title: Great Continental Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo's railway adventure reaches the Dalmatian coastal city of Zadar, once an important Venetian trading hub and still a centre for the production of a Venetian delicacy, Maraschino cherry liqueur. In Sibenik, Michael finds one of the first towns in the world to enjoy electric street lighting.

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Looking at coastal villages that have often been on the front line of history.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.sub-title: Technology and AI for Social Inclusion
programme.desc: Mathematician and Author Hannah Fry explores the powerful new technologies set to shape society, and whether that future is one that we want. Our future doesn't just happen, we make it.

programme.title: The Secret Genius of Modern Life
programme.desc: Hannah hits the production line with appliance giants Bosch to find the lifeblood at the heart of every fridge and gets a lesson in carrot droop.

programme.title: Springwatch
programme.desc: Based in a different location around the UK each spring, 'Springwatch' has a look at the lives of wild animals in their natural habitats, showcasing the beauty and diversity of wildlife around the country.

programme.title: Surgeons: At the Edge of Life
programme.desc: A surgeon has one chance to tackle a life-threatening tumour in his patient's liver and diaphragm. A neurosurgeon must save her patient from paralysis with unerring precision.

programme.title: On The Front Line
programme.sub-title: Exposed: The High Street Crime Crisis
programme.desc: Class A drugs, illegal cigarettes and vapes: just some of the items for sale on the British high street. We go undercover to expose the under-the-counter crime sweeping the country.

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Insights and global interviews on the news stories of the day. With Victoria Derbyshire.

programme.title: A Life in Ten Pictures
programme.desc: A look behind the popular narrative of David Beckham's career to examine a deeper story: how the footballer used his image to turn himself into a global brand.

programme.title: Romeo Akbar Walter
programme.desc: A bank cashier, Romeo, is recruited by India's intelligence agency for espionage in Pakistan. However, he finds himself in challenging situations when his cover gets blown.

programme.title: Professor
programme.desc: Sita devi is a very strict aunt for a number of young ladies, and does not allow them to fraternise with males. Along comes Pritam disguised as an elderly Professor Khanna to break every possible rule of the aunt.

programme.title: Filler
programme.desc: Bringing the best in television.

programme.title: Mere Do Anmol Ratan
programme.desc: A new mother takes home two boys instead of one because of a hospital mixup.

programme.desc: An extraordinary true story based on the life of Mahavir Singh Phogat and his two daughters, Geeta and Babita. An inspirational journey of a father who trains his daughters to become world class wrestlers.

programme.title: Ishq
programme.desc: Four friends go against their parents' wishes and marry for love instead of money.

programme.title: Gabbar Is Back
programme.desc: Gabbar creates a vigilante military network to eliminate corrupt officials. As pressure mounts on the police to find out who is responsible for the killings, a special investigator is summoned to track them down.

programme.title: Inquilaab
programme.desc: A wife becomes suspicious of her police-officer-turned-politician husband and discovers that he is being manipulated by skilled politicians to carry out their nefarious deeds.

programme.desc: The host interviews various media personalities, shares the latest news of the Bollywood industry and reviews the newest movies.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.desc: Mum's friend Val moves in and causes havoc when she finds that Dad is hiding a tin of 20-year-old meat in his shed.

programme.title: Friday Night Dinner
programme.desc: It's Mum's birthday and she's having a surprise party for 44 guests. Only snag is - she's secretly organised it herself! The boys waste no time in teasing her.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: When Jeremy's aunt dies, his mother comes to visit for the funeral. Unfortunately, her new boyfriend is a military man who Jeremy deeply resents, and who Mark immediately idolises. Desperate to impress, Mark endures an extraordinary ordeal.

programme.title: Peep Show
programme.desc: Jeremy and Super Hans get a new band manager and are forced to play a Christian rock festival. Mark comes along as their roadie, and learns how to have sex properly for the first time in his life.

programme.title: Tattoo Fixers on Holiday
programme.desc: Glen meets drunk tattoo collector Jack, Sketch rids Dylan of a reminder of his past, Jay gives Julie a portrait to cherish and Joel loses his dodgy band tattoo.

programme.title: Taskmaster Bleeped
programme.desc: Andy Zaltzman, Babátúndé Aléshé, Emma Sidi, Jack Dee and Rosie Jones compete one last time, hoping for the points that will see them crowned by Greg Davies as the eighteenth 'Taskmaster' champion.

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts as Sean Lock and David Mitchell go up against Jon Richardson and Katherine Ryan. The Brett Domino Trio join Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Prince Charles and Lady Di's impending nuptials inspire Beverly to plan a vow renewal. Barry hopes his band will win him Lainey's attention.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Adam accidentally gets Barry kicked out of a group of cool kids at school. Beverly is upset when she finds out that Erica hangs out with a friend's mother.

programme.title: Don't Tell the Bride: Revisited
programme.sub-title: The Wedding That Was Nearly Cancelled
programme.desc: All the way back in 2011, Anna turned wedding planner for her girly bride-to-be Sian. It wasn't exactly plain sailing, as Anna sent Sian on one of the worst hen nights 'Don't Tell the Bride' has ever seen.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Imran and Leah send shockwaves through the village as they return hand-in-hand. Elsewhere, Cleo seeks encouragement on her first day back at work, and a resident chooses forgiveness.

programme.title: Hollyoaks
programme.desc: Fed up with his family's feuding, Jack pleads with Beth. Leah throws a welcome home party for herself and Imran. And two residents get closer.

programme.title: Rude(ish) Tube
programme.desc: A short, cheeky clip from Rude Tube's naughty little sister Rude-ish Tube, featuring sheep.

programme.title: Couples Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Colchester, touchy-feely Yvonne and Neil compete against fitness fanatics Darren and Amy, and social worker Tony, who makes less-than-complimentary comments about his partner Victoria.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to country music's spiritual home, Nashville, Tennessee, but his visit begins on a sour note at Chappy's Cajun restaurant. Chef John `Chappy' Chapman's careless methods have hurt his local reputation.

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon heads to Scottsdale, Arizona, where he visits Amy's Baking Company and meets husband-and-wife bistro owners Samy and Amy Bouzaglo, who reveal a shocking contempt for customer feedback.

programme.title: Below Deck Mediterranean
programme.desc: The deck team struggles to find their footing, and big mistakes are made; a stew begins to flirt with all male crew members on board; the chef struggles to get a handle on eccentric guest requests; tension between two deckhands escalates.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Foolproof Cooking
programme.desc: Mary cooks up a celebration of six delicious dishes perfect for outdoor entertaining and lazy summer days. To start, she prepares a luxurious whole sea bass with lime and dill butter that couldn't be simpler.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Foolproof Cooking
programme.desc: Mary prepares a simple and easy herb flatbread that the whole family can muck in with. Next up, she enlists the help of two of her older granddaughters to prepare a delicious chicken and bacon lattice pie, with foolproof lattice pastry.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree works on great new ideas for cooking low carb! She prepares grilled pork tenderloin with baby bok choy, cauliflower pizza crust and spicy pizza sauce, roasted shrimp with cherry tomatoes and roasted lemon-chili broccoli.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: There's a new baby on the ranch, so Ree's making a new-mum care package full of dishes she was given and plenty of pampering goodies. She includes brisket, funeral potatoes, angel food cake and fruit salad with honey and thyme.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Fettuccine and Black Bean Beef Burgers
programme.desc: With fettuccine, smoked trout, asparagus and peas, black bean beef burgers, noodles and pickle salad.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie's rustling up pork marsala, porcini rice and spring greens, plus Koh Samui salad, chilli tofu and Thai noodles.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Violinist Itzhak Perlman joins Ina Garten for cooking and conversation, and Ina makes a Potato Galette with Smoked Salmon; Itzhak shares his foolproof Scrambled Eggs with Caramelized Onions to pair with Ina's Strawberries Romanoff.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Designer Daniel Roseberry spends the day with Ina Garten, sharing fashion secrets and flower arrangements, after Ina bakes a Tri-Berry Ricotta Cake for breakfast; then, Ina teaches Daniel how to make smashed burgers with caramelized onions.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nation's most famous tent, in a bid to be crowned star baker.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Rachel bakes a delicious iced orange cake with freshly squeezed orange juice in the icing, an all-American pie, and a spinach, potato and goat's cheese tart.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: No adventure around the UK would be complete without a trip to the capital city, London. James is checking out some of his old haunts and stopping in on Clare Smyth's brand new restaurant Core for a bite to eat and a catch up with the world's best female chef. Then high up on the rooftops of London, he cooks up spiced pigeon breast with dhal. Next, James ventures into south east London to a knife forgery in Peckham. He gets hands on in the process of making Japanese style knives.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James is bringing his culinary tour of the UK to his neck of the woods as he spends precious time in his adopted home county of Hampshire. He's taking his gun dog Cooper to a training session at Laverstoke before cooking an amazing seasonal dish of partridge with vegetables and a sloe gin sauce. James hangs out with his best friend Jody Scheckter, an ex-formula one racer with an epic car collection, and a surprising passion for buffalo mozzarella.

programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a soul food spot in Washington, DC, a specialist hotdog place in Kansas City, and a seafood shack in Massachusetts.

programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a Mediterranean spot in Tennessee, a cafe in Minnesota with wild rice French toast, and has turkey in Kentucky.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Florida, Adam visits an English-style seaside eatery and an Amish restaurant.

programme.title: Man v Food
programme.desc: In Las Vegas, can Adam manage to put away a six pound, two foot long Burrito?

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Buddy is tasked with one of his weirdest requests - baking a South African beef jerky cake! Meanwhile, the team works on music box-themed cake that gets them dancing.

programme.title: Cake Boss
programme.desc: Mauro and Madeline are celebrating their 25th wedding anniversary, so Buddy and Mauro recreate the cake Buddy Sr. did 25 years ago - and now, Mauro's son takes on best man duties.

programme.title: Nigella Bites
programme.desc: A step by step guide to creating delicious dishes for every occasion. Domestic goddess Nigella Lawson shows us how to create great food in a very short space of time - perfect for those of us who spend our lives haring about.

programme.title: Nigella Bites
programme.desc: Why sharing quality time and good food with friends is more important than worrying about the meal's presentation.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Foolproof Cooking
programme.desc: Mary shares her foolproof recipes for those days when you are simply run off your feet. Her mantra is "get organised, and try to do things ahead" and tonight the dishes she prepares are ones you can get together in no time. Tomato soup made from ingredients from the store cupboard, and express bacon and pesto pasta are perfect examples of dishes that take under 20 minutes to rustle up.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Foolproof Cooking
programme.desc: Mary showcases her dishes that are perfect for a party. Sometimes cooking for a crowd can be very daunting, but Mary loves sharing her ideas that are fun, different and don't take forever to prepare. First up is a Thai chicken curry, perfect for a party and all people need is a fork and a bowl! Mary loves a classic dish, and what better way to showcase this than with her ham hock terrine.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's dinner for two with Ree and her husband, Ladd, who's turning into a cowboy cook in the kitchen! They whip up fried shrimp with cocktail sauce, spicy curly fries, grilled corn casserole and Mexican chocolate sundaes.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: It's mash-up mania on the ranch, and Ree proves two is better than one! She merges Italian and Mexican in chicken enchilasagne, then creates courgette bread waffles, potato chip and chocolate chip cookies, and a cheeseburger salad.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.sub-title: Crispy Pork and Grilled Mushroom Sub
programme.desc: On the menu are crispy Parma pork, minted courgettes and brown rice, plus grilled mushroom sub, smoky pancetta, melted cheese and pears.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie cooks sizzling chicken fajitas, grilled peppers, salsa, rice and beans, and prawn cocktail, king prawns and sundried pan bread.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Award-winning musician Jon Batiste joins Ina Garten for a memorable conversation about staying strong and making Grammy history. Ina makes Peanut Butter and Jelly Bars for a taste of nostalgia, and Jon shares his Ma's Red Beans and Rice.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Allison Janney joins Ina Garten at the barn for a day of amazing conversation and a cooking lesson. Ina welcomes Allison with Baked Fontina, a spin on a childhood favourite, and shares the secrets to making perfect Cacio e Pepe and Lemon Vinaigrette.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: It's the semi-final and the remaining bakers take on more challenges set by judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith in the nation's most famous tent, for a place in the final.

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Rachel shows us a fantastic recipe for Baklava, made up of sweet layers of filo pastry, pistachio nuts and almonds.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: Following his French and American Adventures, James Martin explores the British culinary offerings.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Journalist Michael Barbaro joins Ina Garten for Spicy Hermit Bars and conversation about storytelling tactics, style, and the success of his podcast, The Daily. Then, Ina shows Michael an updated Chicken Marbella that's perfect for easy entertaining.

programme.title: Be My Guest With Ina Garten
programme.desc: Hoda Kotb joins Ina Garten to talk about memories and game-changing moments. Ina shows Hoda how to assemble an Easy Mezze Board with Marinated Herbed Feta that is perfect for effortless entertaining, and they make the best Fried Chicken Sandwiches.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off

programme.title: Rachel Allen: Bake!
programme.desc: Rachel shows us a fantastic recipe for Baklava, made up of sweet layers of filo pastry, pistachio nuts and almonds.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure
programme.desc: James is bringing his culinary tour of the UK to his neck of the woods as he spends precious time in his adopted home county of Hampshire. He's taking his gun dog Cooper to a training session at Laverstoke before cooking an amazing seasonal dish of partridge with vegetables and a sloe gin sauce. James hangs out with his best friend Jody Scheckter, an ex-formula one racer with an epic car collection, and a surprising passion for buffalo mozzarella.

programme.title: James Martin's Great British Adventure

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a pub in Philadelphia for fried empanadas, a spot in Washington for stuffed pork chops, and a seafood joint in Maine.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits America's most interesting and unique restaurants. Guy visits a Venezuelan joint in Virginia for a tasty sandwich, an Italian place in Nashville and bratwurst spot in California.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: This trip Guy's chasing down the holiday meal every which way. In Portland, Maine, we visit a family-run joint where stuffed turkey and stuffed lobster are Thanksgiving traditions.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the dessert course and one chef will go home. Phil Carmichael is determined it won't be him but not everything is going to plan when he tackles his 'Cup of Tea for Ma'am'. One of the accompaniments to his earl grey panna cotta just won't work. He has to make a difficult decision.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Wales are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, blogger and food writer Rosie Birkett.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three newcomers are competing to become regional champion. Daniel Smith, a former head chef for the Roux brothers, now runs his own restaurant in his beloved home county of Norfolk. Andrew Scott's worked with the best in the business, and he's concentrating on telling powerful stories as well as delivering perfect flavours. Danny Gill is a former head chef at Daniel Clifford's two star restaurant Midsummer House.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and the chefs are using some of the UK's best seafood: lobster, salmon and Cornish crab. Danny Gill is a chef who likes to use the very latest equipment to create the most intense flavours. He's going to great lengths to maximise the taste of his cucumber consommé and it is causing a lot of interest in the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Two chefs are cooking venison. Danny Gill is making a venison wellington, inspired by his Dad, a former RAF chef who has served up Wellington for royalty at many air force banquets. While Andrew Scott has sourced his venison from the Queen's Scottish estate, Balmoral.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This week three chefs from the central region are fighting to be crowned champion. One competitor will go home, only the two highest scoring chefs will make it through to the judging chamber. Danny Gill is facing his toughest day yet, choosing to make a staggering eight elements for his imaginative dessert.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central region's two highest scoring chefs are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort, for the very first time. Joining them in the chamber is guest judge Enam Ali MBE, a food entrepreneur who set up The British Curry Awards.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the most exciting talents from London and the South East region are competing for a place in the National Finals. Head chef Mark Froydenlund runs Marcus Wareing's two star restaurant. Russell Bateman is head Chef at The Grove in Hertfordshire and Ronnie Murray who heads up the Hix Restaurant group.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and chef Russell Bateman is celebrating the diversity of his beloved home town of London - by combining lobster with jerk spices for an ambitious fish course with seven key elements. Ronnie Murray's taking a risk with an ingredient which is close to the veteran judge's heart...and is planning to serve it at three different temperatures warm, deep fried and frozen. And two Michelin starred Mark Froydenlund is planning a novel way to present his dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the all-important main course and returning contender Mark Froydenlund is banking on it being his strongest dish. Russell Bateman is creating a beef dish, and Ronnie Murray's mixing a crown of lamb with a curried filling.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the dessert course and the final chance for three of London & South East's most competitive and talented chefs to get through to the judging chamber, today the veteran judge will send one chef home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head and the two highest scoring chefs from the London and South East region are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort. Joining this week as guest judge is Kevin Gould, an award winning food writer.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mini Patel is back for a second attempt at the north east regional title but he is competing against two young newcomers, Tommy Banks and Chris Archer. Last year, Mini went home after the dessert course, so he is hoping to keep better control of his nerves this year.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the fish course and two chefs, Tommy and Mini, have very similar ingredients - both are cooking mackerel. But Mini is trying an original beetroot sorbet while Tommy is using wild woodruff and oyster leaf from his own garden. Chris Archer has chosen the king of the sea, turbot, but there is controversy in the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course of the north east region and overnight there has been a dramatic development. It is a tough and emotional day for the chefs as they react to the news but they battle on in a bid to present a perfect dish with venison and wagyu-angus beef - the meat of the day.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is a highly emotional day for one of the chefs as he prepares a dish which is inspired by and a tribute to his own great Briton, his late grandfather. But all is not going to plan and the chef ends up making one key element three times in a bid to achieve perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two chefs are cooking for judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton for the very first time. Joining them as guest judge is John Williams MBE, the executive head chef at The Ritz, the only hotel to hold a royal warrant for banqueting.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is Chris McGowan's third time however, he is facing tough competition from Mark Abbott, head chef at Daniel Clifford's 2 Michelin star restaurant, Midsummer House, and also from Eddie Attwell who has just completed a year as head chef at Ardtara Country House in Upperlands.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mark Abbott is pushing himself again plating up a complex fish course of the very finest produce. He is calling it Shellsational but at the last minute he forgets a key element. Returning chef Chris McGowan is refining cod and chips and Eddie Attwell is getting classical with the king of the sea, turbot.The repartee is relentless as the chefs jostle for top place on the leader board. Once again there is plenty of emotion as the veteran chef delivers the scores.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the main course and returning Chef Chris McGowan is feeling confident but part of his dish involves making his own venison sausages to serve with a venison rack, and getting it all done in time is straining his emotions. Eddie Attwell is also serving the meat but with a traditional Northern Irish dish called Dock pudding. Mark Abbott is pushing himself again, he is using wagyu beef and serving it with tongue and corn cooked in a variety of ways.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking their desserts and one chef is on course for a historic score, he could beat his boss - a veteran of GBM - and achieve more marks than anyone has done throughout the history of the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the head to head. The two highest scoring chefs from Northern Ireland are cooking for judges Oliver Peyton, Prue Leith and Matthew Fort who are joined by guest judge, restaurant critic and Editor-at-Large of the Independent, Amol Rajan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is the national finals of `Great British Menu'. Before cooking starts the eight champions are called to London's Southbank to get a sight of the prize, the chance to cook at a banquet held at the Palace of Westminster, in celebration of everyday Great Britons honoured by the Queen. It has a huge impact on every one of the chefs, each is determined to do all he can to get a dish to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's day two of the national finals, and the eight champions cook their fish courses. This year's challenge has been to produce dishes that are a lasting legacy to the New Elizabethan Age. They must showcase the transformation in British cuisine during the Queen's historic 64 year reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is main course day, the one course every chef wants to cook at the banquet. Judging the chefs' dishes are Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton and guest judge John Williams MBE, executive chef of The Ritz.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It's the final day of the competition, and the last chance for the eight champions to get one of their dishes to the banquet for Queen Elizabeth ll's Great Britons. Judges Matthew Fort, Prue Leith and Oliver Peyton will judge the dessert course and then decide on the banquet menu to be served at the Houses of Parliament. Joining them in the judging chamber is Grace Dent, restaurant critic, journalist and author.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The winning chefs arrive at the Palace of Westminster to cook for the Banquet of Great Britons. The chefs have been selected because their dishes showcase contemporary British cuisine and demonstrate the transformation in British food during the Queen's historic reign.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East - Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Competing for the top spot are 3 new chefs to the competition representing some of the most exciting culinary talent from London, Paul Walsh, Luke Selby and Ben Marks.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: London and South East Mains and Desserts
programme.desc: The three chefs are halfway through their heat and the pressure is mounting. Only two will proceed to Friday's judging and have a chance to represent the London and the South East region at the regional finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining London chefs must cook their four course menus again. The chefs need to impress the formidable judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.sub-title: North East Starter and Fish Courses
programme.desc: Three chefs new to the competition compete in the North East heats. Three of the region's chefs battle it out for the topspot: Mike Carr, Samira Effa, and Tom Anglesea. The three chefs must present the menu of their careers, inspired by 50 years of British pop music.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs are halfway through their heat and will serve up their main courses and desserts. The stakes are high and again they are each serving a unique take on the pop music brief. Mike is confident about his northern dish, `Rack n Roll', which puts both classic Yorkshire hogget and traditional Lancashire hotpot in the spotlight.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North East chefs must cook their four course menus again for the judges: food writer Matthew Fort, restaurateur Oliver Peyton and broadcaster and cook Andi Oliver as well as a guest judge, one of the British pop industry's finest.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth'. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape'.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomer Kray brings an urban edge to proceedings, with a starter inspired by Grime Music called `Fire in the Booth'. It combines hot sauce, veal sweetbreads and tuna belly. Returner Sabrina is compiling a dish of her favourite ingredients, including pasta and ox cheek, for her Italian inspired starter called `My Mixtape'.

programme.title: Great British Menu

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Uncovers the motives behind the people and organisations who made the choice to aid the Nazi war effort.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Viktor Arajs formed a Latvian commando unit that hunted Jews.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: If Léon Degrelle, a writer and journalist, had lived in another era, then he could have been a hero and a leader. However, he became a supporter of Hitler and ended up betraying his faith, his country and his people.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of 'Operation Barbarossa'. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of 'Operation Barbarossa'. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: The ongoing battles in Manchuria, China, Abyssinia, and Spain each serve as a terrible precedent in their own way.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: As the war consumes economic, agricultural and industrial resources, the extent of the war is realised across Europe, Asia and the Pacific.

programme.title: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how people and resources became absorbed in the war efforts by occupying forces during WWII.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores how civilians became defenders against targets of aerial warfare, and the strategic bombing campaigns utilised in an attempt to end the war.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: The cloak of war played a role in the genocide of WWII, allowing unfathomable horrors against innocent women, children, and men. Even within Allied nations, individuals were seen as a potential enemies and denied liberty.

programme.title: WWII: Total War
programme.desc: Explores the aftermath of WWII in the wake of destruction and liberation, with tens of millions dead and millions more displaced.

programme.title: The Mistake that Killed Hitler
programme.desc: In April 1945, as Stalin's Red Army approach from the east and the western Allied forces quickly approach through France and Belgium, Adolf Hitler awaits his fate in his bunker, reflecting on the mistakes that lost him the war.

programme.title: Culture of Memory: Germany's Post-WW2 Statues and Memorials
programme.desc: Germany's 'Erinnerungskultur honors victims of Nazi crimes, shaping a unique post-WWII memorial culture.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Croat Dinko Sakic ran an extermination camp in Axis-occupied Yugoslavia.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: The story of Vidkun Quisling, who declared himself 'Fuhrer' of occupied Norway.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: While the IRA never shared fascist ideology, a number of members traded intelligence with Abwehr as they sought an end to British rule in Ireland.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Historians are divided over Mohammad Amin al-Husayni, Grand Mufti of Jerusalem during WWII. While some say he was unfairly demonised, others point to his ties with fascist Europe.

programme.title: The Strange World of the German Dictator
programme.desc: Explore the complexities of the man who would become one of history's most infamous figures. From his obscure beginnings in Austria to his meteoric rise as Germany's Führer, dive deep into the forces that shaped Hitler's ideology and his leadership.

programme.title: War Comes to America
programme.desc: Director Frank Capra's World War II "Why We Fight" series ends with this portrait of the people.

programme.title: Wings: World War Two in the Skies
programme.desc: In the Second World War, a new generation of planes and daredevil pilots take aerial combat to deadly new heights.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Ancient Dead
programme.desc: Explorers uncover one of the largest Incan burial sites at Ancon on the Peruvian coast. It may be proof that the Chacoans were a matriarchal society.

programme.title: Mysteries of the Ancient Dead
programme.desc: In 1928, an earthquake shift uncovers a row of painted warriors: seven-foot statues encasing mummies from an ancient civilisation; curious bone arrangements in Winterborne are somehow linked to intermingled ashes from dig sites in Slovenia.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of Cardiff castle over more than 2,000 years and reveals how it became transformed from a strategically important Roman and Norman stronghold to a lavish fairy-tale Victorian home.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: In medieval England, the North was ruled from York. Dan Jones explores the history of its castle, from the Viking Jorvik and the Normans, through the dark days of the anti-Jewish pogrom and the imprisonment of the Knights Templar, to one of its most colourful inmates - the legendary highwayman Dick Turpin.

programme.title: Britain's Secret Islands
programme.desc: An exploration of Rathlin Island, including a visit to a Neolithic stone tool factory, a unique upside-down lighthouse, and a WWII shipwreck.

programme.title: Britain's Secret Islands
programme.desc: A trip through England's south coast, diving with blue sharks, exploring the battlements of Drake's Island, learning the origins of Scouts, and checking out fossils on the Isle of Wight.

programme.title: Deadman's Curse
programme.desc: Adam, Kru and Tony follow the path of a failed 1982 Vancouver news team led by a Slumach gold hunter and mystic dowser.

programme.title: Deadman's Curse
programme.desc: After aborting his mission last season, lone wolf prospector David Muise returns undeterred and looking for a partner.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts celebrates the biggest and best archaeological discoveries of 2018 from the north of the UK, starting with a prehistoric Pompeii at the Black Loch of Myrton.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: We join a team as they undertake the largest maritime investigation since the Mary Rose and reveal the extraordinary story of HMS Invincible.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: In medieval England, the North was ruled from York. Dan Jones explores the history of its castle, from the Viking Jorvik and the Normans, through the dark days of the anti-Jewish pogrom and the imprisonment of the Knights Templar, to one of its most colourful inmates - the legendary highwayman Dick Turpin.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon family's manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Cheltenham, Britain's most Regency town, Professor Alice Roberts attends a period society ball, investigates social hierarchy and explores the Regency fondness for enemas.

programme.title: Britain's Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Belfast, the UK's most Victorian city, Alice Roberts visits the shipyards that built the Titanic, gets locked up in the Crumlin Road gaol and learns about 19th-century etiquette.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes up from Lyme Regis on Dorset's Jurassic Coast to the Somerset Levels in the footsteps of a ragtag rebel army led by the dashing young Duke of Monmouth, the illegitimate son of Charles II.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. On three occasions, Highland armies, assisted by the French and the Spanish, attempted to overthrow the King and put a Stuart back on the throne.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Skeletons tell of both violent death and tenderness at the royal seat of power in Bamburgh, Northumbria, while at a remarkable community project at a shopping centre in Sittingbourne, people curate the grave goods of their own ancestors.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the final episode Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of the Tudor age, a time that saw momentous changes across all aspects of British life. Along the way Alice visits excavations at Shakespeare's first theatre in London's Shoreditch, where the Bard began his career and `Romeo and Juliet' was first performed. Alice also joins a team sifting through Shakespeare's rubbish at his last home in Stratford-Upon-Avon.

programme.title: Deadman's Curse

programme.title: Deep Water Salvage

programme.title: Walking Through History

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a house in Guildford, Martin Roberts falls in love with a Grade II-listed house in Torquay in Devon, and Dion Dublin is in Barry in Wales assessing all the work needed on a mid-terrace house. Will the developers' plans run smoothly?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel talks to a 20-year-old developer whose 4th property is in Madeley Heath, Dion meets two friends refurbishing a flat in Birkenhead, and Martin talks to identical twin brothers who have ambitious plans for a derelict fire station in Paisley.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Callington in Cornwall, a three-bed semi in Willenhall near Wolverhampton and a terraced house in Hertfordshire's Watford are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners, and hear their plans for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander talks to experienced developers who deliver a stunning renovation on a subsidence-troubled semi in Coulsdon in Surrey, Dion Dublin meets two brothers with ambitious plans for a commercial property in Derby, and Martel Maxwell meets with two golfing friends turned developers who plan to turn round a one-bedroom flat in Telford in Shropshire in record time.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bed detached house ripe for extending in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a cosy cottage in Nettlestead Green in Kent and a terraced house in Etruria in Stoke-on-Trent without a bathroom or an inside loo are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A garage with development potential in Margate in Kent, a ground-floor flat in a Victorian house in Shepherd's Bush, London, and a piece of land ripe for building on in Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a retired bingo caller who buys a renovation project in Maidstone, and Martin has to navigate his way through a maze of a building to find a one-bedroom flat in Salford and calls in on a house in Stoke with an outside loo.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy looks round a rundown two-bedroom flat in Fulham, and Martin visits a former railway cottage in the North East and a terraced house near the Kent coast that needs doing up.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a holiday home on the south coast, a detached house in the Midlands and a great tenement flat with much potential in Glasgow.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin visits a two-bedroom house in Stoke with wonderful countryside views and a great ground floor flat in Glasgow, while Lucy meets two boyhood friends who join forces to buy and renovate a house in north London.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander tours a farmhouse and 10-acre equestrian centre in Kent, and Martin Roberts is in the midlands and the north looking at more modest renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Lucy Alexander visit three houses near water in different parts of the country, including a wonderful cottage with a stunning view in Devon, a town house in Sheerness and a charming one-bedroom cottage in North Shields.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: It is a family affair as a father and daughter, a mum and her son and two cousins tackle very different renovation projects, from an end terrace to a plot of land.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander is serenaded by an opera singer who property develops in his spare time, and Martin Roberts is in the Midlands and Cumbria meeting a couple and a young man, both determined to make money from their property investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets three friends who give a top floor flat in Harrow a high-end finish, and Martin meets a seasoned property developer in Durham and a PhD student in Stoke on Trent who both hope to make money on their three bedroom property investments.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy has a look around a former funeral directors in Maidstone and a two-bedroom purpose built flat in Cricklewood, while Martin catches up with a young man who took up property developing in Stoke on Trent after a motorcycle accident.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts looks around a semi in Northern Ireland and a flat in Newcastle, while Lucy Alexander has a look at a basement flat in Dover which boasts a stunning view.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Inspector Morse

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When convicted murderer Grady Felton returns to the village where he killed a boy years earlier, his arrival is met with anger from the locals. programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The remains of an eight-year-old boy are uncovered, twenty-five years after he was reported missing. DS Brooks and Devlin re-interview the man arrested during the original enquiry. The boy's childhood friend reluctantly agrees to undergo EMDR therapy and appears to recall the events of the night that Tommy disappeared, but George Castle is sceptical about the value of her recovered memories.

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: When an arson attack on a Turkish club claims 17 lives, DS Ronnie Brooks and Matt Devlin are under pressure to discover just who was behind it. Their search is complicated by a trail of false identities, and the devious method of starting the fire, but when the arsonist is finally identified, James Steel and Alesha Phillips struggle to build a case. They begin to question whether the attack was really racially motivated.
